title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:Mac:kernel-workspace:39719:run:20260404T184044:43804 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/kernel/bootstrap...
summary_text: - High: `/kernel memory-query` is not wired to the new subcommand. `scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh` maps every `/kernel*` token to `codex-prompt-launch kernel` ([file](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh#L66)), and `tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh` only verifies `/kernel` and `/vote`, not `/kernel memory-query` ([file](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh#L66)). A common Codex slash-prompt path will therefore still enter the Kernel prompt instead of the memory query route. Fix: add an explicit `memory-query` branch in the orchestrator shim or prompt launcher, plus a regression test for `codex exec -C <repo> /kernel memory-query ...`. - Medium: The top-level entrypoint path lacks a regression test for the new subcommand. `scripts/local/launchers/kernel` does pass through `memory-query` to `codex-kernel-guard` ([file](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L22)), but `tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh` only exercises auto-open/new/prompt fallback behavior and never invokes `memory-query` ([file](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh#L108)). Fix: add an entrypoint case that invokes `kernel memory-query ...` and asserts the guard path is reached.
